definition_origin: The choice of asset (illustration, photo, icon, motion, sound) is itself a brand-archetype signal — communicating personality before any copy loads. Rooted in Carl Jung's 12 universal archetypes (Innocent, Everyman, Hero, Outlaw, Explorer, Creator, Ruler, Magician, Lover, Caregiver, Jester, Sage), translated to brand identity via Mark & Pearson's 'The Hero and the Outlaw' (2001). The asset itself is the message — a hand-drawn line communicates Caregiver before any word is read; a luminous gradient communicates Magician; a 6-color mark communicates Jester.
core_purpose: At Norman's VISCERAL level (sub-100ms), the user feels the archetype from a single asset — pastels and rounded shapes = nurture; high-contrast monochrome = authority; neon arcs = energy. At BEHAVIORAL level, asset choice trains the user on what kind of interaction to expect (organic shapes invite touch; sharp geometry signals efficiency). At REFLECTIVE level, consistent archetype-anchored asset choice builds brand recognition and pride-of-association. Asset choice is therefore the cheapest, fastest archetype signal — far before microcopy or feature design lands.
when_needed: Mandatory whenever a brand archetype has been chosen — every asset decision should map to it. Especially critical at FIRST-IMPRESSION surfaces (app icon, splash, onboarding hero, marketing landing). Less critical for utility-deep B2B dashboards where data legibility outranks personality, but even there choosing Sage-aligned data viz vs Magician-aligned reveals discipline.
format_options: Asset format itself signals archetype: SVG/vector = Creator/Sage/Ruler (precise, scalable, restrained). Hand-drawn raster PNG/WebP = Caregiver/Innocent/Jester (organic, warm). Lottie/JSON animation = Jester/Magician/Hero (motion personality). Rive interactive = Magician/Creator (refined transformation). Photographic JPG = Lover/Everyman/Explorer (authenticity, real life). 3D/glTF = Magician/Hero (advanced, transformative). Variable fonts = Creator/Magician (flexibility). Static type = Ruler/Sage (authority, restraint). Choosing format itself is an archetype declaration.
file_size_performance: Archetype-discipline tightens asset budget: Sage/Ruler restraint = ship fewer, smaller assets (<500KB total); Jester/Magician indulgence = larger Lottie/Rive budgets (1-3MB acceptable when they ARE the experience). Always target <100KB per hero asset, <30KB per icon, <500KB per Lottie unless it carries critical brand moment.
free_tools: Figma (free for archetype design tokens, color palette boards, asset libraries). Penpot (free open-source alternative; native design-tokens). Tokens Studio (free Figma plugin for archetype-locked token export). Midjourney v6/v7 niji6 — paid but nano-banana via Gemini Pro is free for visual moodboarding per archetype. Pinterest (archetype reference moodboards). Coolors / Khroma (palette generation per archetype). Google Fonts (typeface selection per archetype — Crimson Text for Sage, Fraunces for Ruler, Bagel Fat for Jester, Caveat for Caregiver). LottieFiles community library (filter by personality). Mixkit / Lottiefiles for archetype-tagged animation. Atlas Studio + JD Meier Brand Archetype Guide (free articles).
paid_tools: STARTER ($10-15/mo): Figma Professional $15/seat/mo (unlimited library files for archetype guide). PRO ($20-30/mo): Frontify $25/mo (brand guideline hosting). Brand24 (archetype-positioning research). ENTERPRISE ($50+/mo): Toolabs Designsystems Manager. Pay only when team >2 people; solo dev does not need any of these.
gemini_pro_workflow: Gemini Pro is exceptional for archetype-translation: (1) Feed brand archetype + emotional spec, ask 'translate this into 5 design tokens for each domain: color palette (HSL), typography stack, illustration style, motion personality, sound posture, photography direction'. (2) 'Generate a moodboard prompt list (10 Imagen 4 prompts) for the {ARCHETYPE} archetype — each illustrating a different facet (typography, color, illustration, motion, photography, ornament, geometric character, texture, layout, ornament)'. (3) Audit existing UI: paste screenshots and ask 'which archetype is this currently signaling? where does it conflict with stated archetype {X}?'. (4) Use NotebookLM with all 12-archetype reference docs + your brand brief to produce an archetype-locked design system spec. (5) Imagen 4 via Gemini for archetype-specific illustration generation with style references. (6) Whisk (until Apr 2026 retirement) for blending archetype reference images.
workflow_steps: Step 1 — Lock archetype (1 hr): pick primary + secondary from 12 Jungian archetypes; document why with 1-paragraph emotional promise. Step 2 — Reference moodboard (2 hr): pull 30-50 images from Pinterest/Mobbin/Dribbble that exemplify the archetype; tag each with what's working. Step 3 — Translate to design tokens (2 hr in Gemini Pro): color (5 hex), typography (1 display + 1 text), illustration style (3 adjectives + 1 reference artist), motion (timing + easing curves), sound (BPM + instrument), photography (3 adjectives). Step 4 — Build archetype-spec.md (1 hr): document with Do/Don't for each token. Step 5 — Generate sample assets (3-4 hr): one of each — icon, illustration, photo, motion, sound — to validate the spec. Step 6 — Stress-test (1 hr): show 5 strangers; ask 'what's this brand's personality?' — they should hit the archetype within 10s. Step 7 — Lock and version (30 min): commit to repo as archetype-spec.md and tokens.json. Step 8 — Write archetype-prompts.md (1 hr): reusable prompt fragments to plug into AI tools that ALL future assets reference.
ai_prompt_templates: Archetype-locked illustration: '{ARCHETYPE_STYLE_ADJECTIVES} illustration of {SUBJECT}, {ARCHETYPE_PALETTE} palette ({HEX_LIST}), {ARCHETYPE_MOTION_QUALITY} (e.g. soft organic / bold geometric / minimal restrained), {ARCHETYPE_REFERENCE_ARTIST} reference style, no text, transparent background, vector-clean'. Archetype-color generation: 'Generate a 7-color HSL palette for the {ARCHETYPE} archetype: 1 primary, 1 secondary, 1 accent, 4 neutrals; reasoning: each color tied to {ARCHETYPE_EMOTION}'. Audit prompt: 'Audit these UI screenshots against {ARCHETYPE} archetype. Score 0-10 on color, type, illustration, motion, copy. Identify conflicts and propose fixes.' Archetype translation: 'Convert {ARCHETYPE} brand archetype to specific design tokens: 5-color palette with HSL values + emotion mapping, 2 Google fonts (display + body), 3 illustration style adjectives + 1 reference artist, motion easing function (cubic-bezier values) + duration ranges, sound BPM range and instrument list, photography direction (3 adjectives + 3 sample subjects)'.
claude_code_integration: Maintain /apps/mobile/src/design/archetype-spec.md and /packages/shared/src/tokens/{archetype}.json. Use Style Dictionary or tokens-studio export to transform JSON tokens into platform code (RN StyleSheet, Tailwind, CSS vars). Have Claude Code audit pull-requests with a slash-command /archetype-audit that diffs new assets against tokens.json. Build a /scripts/audit-tokens.ts that scans for hardcoded colors / fonts / shadows not in the archetype palette and warns. Wire ESLint rule to forbid raw color literals outside tokens.
emotional_design_integration: INNOCENT: hand-drawn rough edges, pastel pinks/blues/yellows, rounded sans like Quicksand or Nunito, lottie bouncing, gentle ascending bell SFX, photography of children/nature/sunlight. EVERYMAN: stock-feel real photography, neutral grays + 1 accent, Inter or Open Sans, minimal motion, no SFX, candid lifestyle imagery. HERO: bold geometric sans (Anton, Bebas, Druk), saturated red + black + gold, big drum SFX, ascending arrows + power-stance illustrations, action photography. OUTLAW: distressed type (Times Now, Migra), high-contrast black + neon, broken-grid layouts, glitchy SFX, anti-aesthetic photography (raw, lo-fi). EXPLORER: muted earth tones (terracotta, forest, sky), serif-sans pair (Cormorant + Inter), maps + compasses + horizon-line motion, ambient world SFX, landscape photography. CREATOR: minimal monospace (JetBrains Mono, IBM Plex), single luminous accent on neutral, restrained micro-motion, refined click SFX, abstract geometric illustration. RULER: serif display (Playfair, Bodoni, Canela), black/gold/deep-purple, orchestral SFX, marble/architectural photography, formal geometric ornament. MAGICIAN: gradient meshes (used INTENTIONALLY here, not lazy), purple/black/luminous-cyan, evolving Lottie, glassy synth swells, abstract liquid imagery, refined editorial type (GT Sectra, Fraunces). LOVER: warm gradients (rose, peach, terracotta), soft script + serif, slow heartbeat motion, gentle cello/strings SFX, intimate skin-tone photography, candle/silk textures. CAREGIVER: hand-drawn warmth, soft greens/blues/cream, hand-lettered or rounded sans (Recoleta, Sofia Pro Soft), slow lottie, gentle chime SFX, holding-hands photography, organic shapes. JESTER: bright multi-color (yellow, orange, magenta, teal), bold rounded display (Bagel Fat One, DM Serif Display, custom), bouncy spring motion, marimba SFX, exaggerated cartoon mascot, playful wonky photography. SAGE: serif text body (Crimson, Lora), restrained neutrals + single navy/forest accent, broadcast-quality voice-over, data-rich photography, library/archive imagery, almost no animation. Each archetype's asset choice IS the archetype signal before the user reads any word.
specific_examples: Headspace = Jester-leaning Caregiver: orange + hand-drawn smile mascot + bright marimba SFX + Andy Puddicombe's warm voice — every asset choice signals 'meditation can be fun'. https://standards.site/case-studies/headspace/ . Calm = pure Caregiver: soft blue gradient + nature photography + Tamara Levitt voice + zero loud SFX — every asset signals 'I will hold space for you'. Linear = Creator-Magician: monospace + dark canvas + single purple-blue gradient + sharp Lottie microinteractions — every asset signals 'sophisticated craft tool for builders'. https://linear.app/ . Liquid Death = Outlaw: heavy metal type + skull illustrations + tallboy can + irreverent voice — water as anti-water. https://liquiddeath.com/ . Notion = Sage-Creator: Inter type + monochrome + restrained illustration system + minimal motion — every asset signals 'clarity and craft'. Duolingo = Jester: bright green + Duo mascot + bouncy SFX + irreverent push notifications — every asset signals 'playful coercion'. https://design.duolingo.com/ . Apple = Magician-Ruler: Helvetica/SF + gradient hardware photography + tu-tu-rock startup chime + refined glass UI — every asset signals 'transformative craft'.
anti_ai_slop_traps: Most common archetype-MISMATCH from AI generation: (1) AI defaults to Magician aesthetic regardless of prompt — luminous gradients, glassy 3D, neon-on-black — even when you want Sage. Counter: explicitly anti-prompt ('no gradients, no glow, no neon'). (2) AI defaults to Jester-flavored color riot for 'fun' brands — counter with restraint and pick 3 colors max. (3) Stock illustration libraries (unDraw, Storyset) all signal a generic Everyman-Creator hybrid that is now slop signature — read item 23. (4) AI mascot generators default to Disney-Pixar Caregiver chibi — counter with bespoke style references and rough hand-drawn texture. (5) Default Lottie animations from LottieFiles community signal 'corporate Everyman' — modify timing curves, rebuild from scratch when budget allows. Counter universal: define 5 anti-tokens ('this brand will NEVER use X, Y, Z') and bake them into every prompt template.
common_pitfalls: (1) Picking archetype after building visuals (designing against bias instead of with intent). (2) Picking too many archetypes ('we're Hero AND Caregiver AND Sage') — pick 1 primary + 1 secondary maximum. (3) Asset choice contradicts copy ('warm caring brand' with hard geometric icons). (4) Skipping the archetype-spec.md document — every contributor reinvents tokens. (5) Letting trend-chasing override archetype lock (joining the bento-grid trend when your brand is Sage). (6) Forgetting that archetype includes MOTION and SOUND, not just static visuals. (7) Treating 12 archetypes as exclusive instead of dominant + recessive (Calm is Caregiver-dominant with Sage-recessive). (8) Not stress-testing with users — visual self-perception rarely matches stranger-perception.
accessibility_notes: Each archetype's color palette must independently meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI). Caregiver pastels easily fail — pair with darker text reliably. Outlaw's glitch motion can trigger photosensitive seizures — provide reduce-motion alternative. Jester's high-saturation palettes can fatigue color-blind users — never communicate state via color alone; pair with shape/icon. Sage's restrained typography must not go below 16px body. All archetype motion must respect prefers-reduced-motion media query.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Archetype-signal lands faster on mobile due to constrained surface — every pixel works harder. App icon is single largest archetype carrier. Web has more room for archetype texture (background patterns, hero photography, scroll choreography) and richer typography. Mobile motion budget tighter (60-120ms) vs web (200-400ms). Mobile sound a stronger archetype signal because phones are sound-emitting devices; web sound rarer.
sources: [The Ultimate Guide to the 12 Brand Archetypes (JD Meier)](https://jdmeier.com/brand-archetypes/) — comprehensive archetype reference. [Brand Archetypes — A Complete Guide for Designers (Marlo Studios)](https://marlostudios.co/blogs/journal/brand-archetypes-a-complete-guide-for-designers) — design-application focus. [Iconic Fox: Brand Archetypes Definitive Guide with 36 Examples](https://iconicfox.com.au/brand-archetypes/) — examples-driven. [LogRocket: Brand archetype vs persona for UX](https://blog.logrocket.com/ux-design/brand-archetype-vs-persona-how-brand-work-guides-ux-design/) — applied to UX. [Horanin: A brand archetype in visual identity](https://horanin.com/a-brand-archetype-in-visual-identity/) — visual translation. [Norman's Three Levels of Design (IxDF)](https://ixdf.org/literature/article/norman-s-three-levels-of-design) — Visceral / Behavioral / Reflective framing. [Standards.site Headspace Case Study](https://standards.site/case-studies/headspace/) — archetype-driven design system in practice. [Mark & Pearson, The Hero and the Outlaw — original 12 archetypes book](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Hero_and_the_Outlaw).
